report_name: Foxconn North America Hit by Nitrogen Ransomware; 8TB of Data Allegedly Stolen

threat_actors.name: Nitrogen
threat_actors.description: A relatively new but aggressive ransomware group active since late 2024. They claimed responsibility for the attack on Foxconn, employing a double-extortion model involving data encryption and exfiltration.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through data encryption and the threat of leaking sensitive exfiltrated data.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Nitrogen ransomware payload was deployed to encrypt files across Foxconn's network, causing significant operational disruption.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Exfiltration
attack_patterns.description: The Nitrogen group exfiltrated 8 terabytes of data, including over 11 million files containing confidential documents and schematics, from Foxconn's network before deploying the ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely deleted Volume Shadow Copies using commands such as 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet' to hamper recovery efforts.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: The Nitrogen group likely disabled security software on the compromised network to evade detection and allow their tools to run unimpeded.

malware.name: Nitrogen Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Nitrogen group to encrypt files on victim networks, such as Foxconn's North American systems, as part of a double-extortion scheme.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Used by the Nitrogen group for lateral movement and execution of the ransomware payload within the victim's network.

identities.name: Foxconn
identities.description: A Taiwanese multinational electronics contract manufacturer and key supplier for major tech companies. Its North American operations were targeted in a ransomware attack.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose confidential data and schematics were allegedly stolen during the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Google
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose confidential data and schematics were allegedly stolen during the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Intel
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose confidential data and schematics were allegedly stolen during the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Dell
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose sensitive data was allegedly stolen during the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Nvidia
identities.description: A high-profile client of Foxconn whose sensitive data was allegedly stolen during the ransomware attack.

infrastructure.name: Nitrogen Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Nitrogen ransomware group to list victims and threaten the release of exfiltrated data.

infrastructure.name: Cloud Exfiltration Storage
infrastructure.description: Cloud storage services likely used by the Nitrogen group to exfiltrate the 8TB of data stolen from Foxconn.

indicators.name: Nitrogen VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b9a8b3c-1d9e-4f7a-8c6d-5e4f3a2b1c0d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/foxconn-confirms-north-american-factories-hit-by-cyberattack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-14
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, although this is rare.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command used by ransomware actors like Nitrogen to delete volume shadow copies, hampering recovery efforts.

indicators.name: PsExec Execution for Lateral Movement
indicators.pattern:
title: PsExec or PAExec Execution
id: 8d7c6b5a-4e3f-2d1c-0b9a-8c7d6e5f4a3b
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PsExec or its alternative PAExec, which are often abused by attackers for lateral movement and remote execution.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/foxconn-confirms-north-american-factories-hit-by-cyberattack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-14
tags:
  - attack.t1569.002
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate remote administration by system administrators.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    OriginalFileName:
      - 'psexec.exe'
      - 'paexec.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of PsExec, a tool leveraged by the Nitrogen group for lateral movement and payload deployment.
